name: libio-multiplex-perl
title: libio-multiplex-perl
summary: object-oriented interface to select() for Perl
description:
object-oriented interface to select() for Perl
IO::Multiplex is designed to take the effort out of managing
multiple file handles.  It is essentially a really fancy front end to
the select() system call.  In addition to maintaining the select()
loop, it buffers all input and output to/from the file handles.  It
can also accept incoming connections on one or more listen sockets.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libio-multiplex-perl because object-oriented interface to select() for Perl.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libio-multiplex-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libio-multiplex-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libio-multiplex-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
